entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A major legume crop grown in eastern Africa, rich in protein, nitrogen-fixing, and drought tolerant.

entities.name: EASTERN AFRICA
entities.description: A region in Africa where pigeonpea is grown and affected by specific pests and diseases.

entities.name: PROTEIN
entities.description: A nutrient of which pigeonpea is a rich source.

entities.name: NITROGEN-FIXING
entities.description: The ability of the pigeonpea crop to fix nitrogen in the soil.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of pigeonpea to grow in water-limited conditions.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID AREAS
entities.description: Environmental conditions in Africa for which pigeonpea is an ideal crop.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The continent where pigeonpea is grown and attacked by various pests and diseases.

entities.name: PESTS
entities.description: Organisms that attack pigeonpea and act as a constraint to increased production.

entities.name: DISEASES
entities.description: Pathological conditions that attack pigeonpea and act as a constraint to increased production.

entities.name: POD BORER
entities.description: Helicoverpa armigera, the most important pest of pigeonpea worldwide.

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT
entities.description: The most important disease of pigeonpea in eastern Africa.

entities.name: FUSARIUM UDUM
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes Fusarium wilt in pigeonpea.

entities.name: WILT-RESISTANCE
entities.description: A trait being developed by ICRISAT to protect pigeonpea from Fusarium wilt.
